title: GPT-6 Is Launching Into a World OpenAI No Longer Controls
summary: A video essay analyzing OpenAI's competitive decline as Microsoft, Apple, and Google integrate competing AI systems (Project Polaris, Gemini, Claude). The piece covers GPT-6 rumors, the shift to GPT-5.5 release, benchmark competition, and OpenAI's financial and organizational challenges including potential IPO plans and litigation.
writeup: The video argues that GPT-6's delayed release has cost OpenAI its leverage with major platform partners. It cites Microsoft integrating Project Polaris into GitHub Copilot, Apple adopting Gemini, and Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 reportedly outscoring GPT-5.5 on benchmarks. The framing is a shift in competitive positioning: as the launch slips, cloud and device partners diversify away from OpenAI's models, eroding its prior market dominance.
